这是一个直接来自史蒂文·西格尔 (Steven Seagal) 电影的加密骗局，其中包括动作电影明星本人、私家侦探、策划的绑架以及数百万美元消失得无影无踪。检察官表示，这次欺诈让近 500 名投资者损失了超过 1100 万美元的现金和他们投入所谓的加密货币挖矿业务的各种成熟加密货币，并承诺在 60 到 90 天内获得高达 200% 的收益。但联邦检察官和美国证券交易委员会的调查人员表示，这些钱没有获得丰厚回报，而是汇入了 Kristijan Krstic 控制的海外账户。上周，55 岁的加州私家侦探约翰·德马尔 (John DeMarr) 曾担任该骗局的主要负责人，他承认帮助实施欺诈行为，同时将近 200 万美元收入囊中，他曾用这笔钱购买保时捷和宝马，支付还清了超过 100 万美元的债务，并为他在海豹滩的家进行了大规模翻新。当他于 2022 年 1 月 4 日被判刑时，他将面临最高五年的监禁。 在 2 月宣布对德马尔的起诉几天后，德克萨斯州的联邦检察官对 Krstic 和其他 15 名被告展开了更广泛的刑事起诉，其中许多是塞尔维亚人国民，电汇欺诈和洗钱指控。在这种情况下，该组织被指控进行多达 20 次类似的加密货币骗局，骗取投资者高达 7000 万美元。美国证券交易委员会表示，它认为 Krstic 在塞尔维亚被拘留，但他的确切下落不明，因此当它试图以单独的民事诉讼为他服务时，它不得不在塞尔维亚报纸上刊登广告。无法立即联系 Krstic 置评。
调查人员表示，DeMarr 于 2017 年入伍以推广 Start Options，这是一个提供加密货币挖掘、交易和数字资产交易服务的在线投资平台。 SEC 表示，由于 Krstic 过去曾被指控在澳大利亚和加拿大经营庞氏骗局，因此他在该计划中使用了 Felix Logan 的别名，担任 Start Options 的首席财务官。 DeMarr 的律师表示，直到该计划启动一年多后，他的客户才知道 Krstic 的真实身份。该公司声称它在中国经营着巨大的加密矿场，可以在两个月内提供 25% 到 200% 的回报，具体取决于投资的金额。检察官说，投资者收到了虚假陈述，表明他们的资金在增长，尽管事实上它实际上并没有投资任何东西。当两个月过去了，投资者开始要钱时，他们被告知，这笔钱将被转存为一种称为 Bitcoiin2Gen（或 B2G）的新加密货币的首次发行，并承诺他们将免费获得额外的硬币他们投资的一个。美国证券交易委员会表示，虽然投资者被告知他们可以随时退出，但没有人得到退款。调查人员说，DeMarr 随后聘请了一位前律师，他在此次发布时失去了制作新闻稿和技术白皮书的执照，其中大部分内容是从其他成功的加密货币发布的白皮书中复制而来的。美国证券交易委员会表示，DeMarr 开始向投资者承诺新货币将带来 8,000 倍的回报。就在这时，武侠和动作片明星史蒂文·西格尔 (Steven Seagal) 出现在 B2G 的宣传材料中。 “禅宗大师史蒂文西格尔已成为 Bitcoiin2Gen 的品牌大使，”一份新闻稿中写道，其中声称他是新货币的投资者，并且他“全心全意”支持该产品。在社交媒体上，西格尔敦促他当时在 Facebook 上拥有近 700 万粉丝和在 Twitter 上拥有 100,000 名粉丝不要“错过”B2G 机会。
美国证券交易委员会表示，没有说明的是，西格尔获得了担任发言人的报酬，承诺为他提供 250,000 美元和 750,000 美元的 B2G 代币。西格尔后来同意支付超过 300,000 美元的罚款，因为他没有披露他已获得代言报酬，从而非法兜售证券。在达成协议时，西格尔在一份声明中表示，当他开始怀疑“公司的诚意”时，他放弃了这项安排。他说他只收到了 25 万美元费用的一部分。西格尔的经理在一封电子邮件中说，这位动作明星“和其他人一样被骗了”，“史蒂文只是运气不好和时机不好”。根据美国证券交易委员会提交的民事诉讼，B2G 总共为其推出筹集了 720 万美元。 2018 年 3 月，当 Logan (Krstic) 和 Seagal 宣布离开公司时，投资者开始要求退款。调查人员说，在 Krstic 据称完全消失后不久，他控制的账户中约有 700 万美元。检察官说，受到投资者投诉的猛烈抨击，德马尔错误地宣布该公司已被俄罗斯风险基金收购。然后他告诉投资者将他们的 B2G 代币赎回给他，他将前往黑山将它们出售给新所有者，每个人都会拿回他们的钱。法庭文件显示，不久之后，该公司发布了令人震惊的公告，称德马尔在黑山遭到袭击并失踪，投资者应停止尝试与他联系。实际上，德马尔躲在加利福尼亚州奥兰治县的家中。
去年 2 月，DeMarr 被纽约东区的一个联邦大陪审团以一项合谋进行证券欺诈的罪名起诉。
note_en:
It was a crypto scam straight out of a Steven Seagal movie, featuring the action film star himself, a private eye, a staged kidnapping, and millions of dollars that vanished into thin air.
  Prosecutors say the fraud left nearly 500 investors out more than $11 million in cash and a mix of established cryptocurrencies that they had plunked into a purported crypto-mining operation—with the promise of up to 200% gains within 60 to 90 days.
  But instead of reaping huge returns, the money was funneled into accounts overseas controlled by Kristijan Krstic, an accused Serbian-Australian huckster living in the Philippines with a history of fraud allegations, federal prosecutors and investigators with the Securities and Exchange Commission say.
  Last week, John DeMarr, a 55-year-old California private investigator who had served as the frontman for the scam,  pleaded guilty to helping run the fraud while pocketing nearly $2 million, which he used to buy a Porsche and a BMW, pay off over $1 million in debts, and finance a lavish renovation of his Seal Beach home.
  He faces up to five years in prison when he is sentenced on Jan. 4, 2022.
    Days after the indictment against DeMarr was announced in February, federal prosecutors in Texas unsealed a broader criminal indictment against Krstic and 15 other defendants, many of them Serbian nationals, on wire-fraud and money-laundering charges. In that case, the group is accused of running as many as 20 similar cryptocurrency scams that bilked investors out of as much as $70 million.
  The SEC has said it believes Krstic is in custody in Serbia, but his exact whereabouts are unknown, so when it attempted to serve him with a separate civil suit, it had to take out an ad in a Serbian newspaper. Krstic couldn’t immediately be contacted for comment.
  Investigators say DeMarr was enlisted in 2017 to promote Start Options, an online investment platform that provided cryptocurrency mining, trading, and digital asset trading services. Because Krstic had been accused of running Ponzi schemes in Australia and Canada in the past, he assumed the alias of Felix Logan for the scheme, serving as Start Options’ chief financial officer, the SEC said.
   DeMarr’s attorney said his client was unaware of Krstic’s real identity until more than a year after the scheme was launched.
  The outfit purported that it ran giant crypto-mining farms in China and could deliver between 25% and 200% returns within two months, depending on how much money was invested. Investors were provided with false statements showing their money was growing, despite the fact it hadn’t actually hadn’t been invested in anything, prosecutors said.
  When two months had passed and investors began asking for their money, they were told it was being rolled over to the initial offering of a new cryptocurrency  known as Bitcoiin2Gen (or B2G), with the promise that they would receive an additional coin free for every one they had invested. While investors were told they could pull out at any time, no one was given their money back, the SEC said.
  Investigators say DeMarr then hired a former lawyer who had lost his license to craft press releases and technical white papers on the launch, most of which was copied from the white papers of other, successful cryptocurrency launches. DeMarr began promising investors the new currency would deliver 8,000 times return, the SEC said.
    This is when Steven Seagal, star of martial arts and action films like  Under Siege and  Above the Law, made his appearance, surfacing in promotional materials for B2G.
  “Zen Master Steven Seagal Has Become the Brand Ambassador of Bitcoiin2Gen,” read one press release, in which it was claimed he was an investor in the new currency and that he “wholeheartedly” endorsed the offering. On social media, Seagal urged his then-nearly 7 million followers on  Facebook and 100,000 on  Twitter  to not “miss out” on the B2G opportunity.
  What was left unsaid was that Seagal was being paid to be a spokesman, with the promise of $250,000 plus $750,000 in B2G coin for his time, the SEC said.
  Seagal later  agreed to pay more than $300,000 in fines for unlawfully touting a security by failing to disclose he had been paid for his endorsement. At the time of the agreement, Seagal said in a statement that he backed away from the arrangement when he began having doubts about the “bona fides of the company.” He said he was only paid part of the $250,000 fee.
  Seagal’s manager said in an email that the action star “was conned like everyone else,” and that it was “just bad luck and timing for Steven.”
  In all, B2G raised $7.2 million for its launch, according to the civil complaint filed by the SEC.
  In March 2018, when it was announced that Logan (Krstic) and Seagal were leaving the company, investors began clamoring for their money back. Soon after Krstic allegedly disappeared completely with about $7 million in the accounts he had control over, investigators said.
  Bombarded by complaints from investors, DeMarr falsely announced that the company had been acquired by Russian venture funds, prosecutors said. He then told investors to redeem their B2G coins to him and that he would travel to Montenegro to sell them to the new owners and that everyone would get their money back.
  Soon after, the company issued a stunning announcement that DeMarr had been assaulted in Montenegro and had disappeared and that investors should stop trying to contact him, according to court documents. In reality, DeMarr was holed up in his house in Orange County, Calif.
  Last February, DeMarr was indicted by a federal grand jury in the Eastern District of New York on one count of conspiring to commit securities fraud.
title: 这个加密骗局使投资者损失了 1100 万美元，并付钱给动作明星史蒂文·西格尔（Steven Seagal）进行宣传
title_en: This Crypto Scam Bilked Investors Out of $11 Million—and Paid Action Star Steven Seagal to Promote It

note: 现在，一家新的初创公司希望成为 UX 团队的关键 DesignOps 平台，并已筹集资金帮助其扩大规模。 Zeroheight 现在已经筹集了 1000 万美元的 A 轮融资，由 Tribe Capital 领投，Adobe、Y Combinator、FundersClub 和 Expa 以及天使投资人包括 Tom Preston-Werner（GitHub 联合创始人）、Bradley Horowitz（副总裁）跟投。 Google 产品）、Irene Au（为 Google 构建和运行 UX 设计）和 Nick Caldwell（Twitter 工程副总裁）。总部位于伦敦的 zeroheight 现在将扩展到旧金山/湾区，并全面发展团队。到目前为止，它的重点一直是用户体验文档，但它现在还将探索其他领域，例如缩小设计和开发之间的差距。联合创始人杰罗姆·德·拉法格 (Jerome de Lafargue) 表示：“zeroheight 为 UX 所做的事情就像 GitHub 等 DevOps 平台为构建和交付代码所做的那样，提供了一个记录和管理 UX 组件的中心位置，再加上允许团队跳过设计交接的设计 API并加快用户体验交付过程。”他说该公司解决了用户体验团队的扩展问题：“问题已经出现，因为用户体验团队在过去几年中急剧增长，因为用户体验现在对于大多数公司来说非常重要，只是为了竞争。因此，因此您现在需要集中化，您需要可重复使用的组件，以便团队可以提高效率，而不会在不断发货时降低质量。” Zeroheight 拥有 1,300 多个客户群，其中包括 Adob​​e 和 United Airlines 等多家财富 500 强公司。
note_en:
Now, a new startup hopes to become a key DesignOps platform for UX teams, and has raised money to help it, in turn, scale-up.
 Zeroheight has now raised a $10 million Series A funding round led by Tribe Capital, with participation from Adobe, Y Combinator, FundersClub and Expa, as well as angel investors including Tom Preston-Werner (co-founder of GitHub), Bradley Horowitz (VP Product at Google), Irene Au (built and ran UX design for Google) and Nick Caldwell (VP Engineering at Twitter).
 London-based zeroheight will now expand to the San Francisco/Bay Area, and grow the team across the board. Its focus so far has been on UX documentation, but it will now also explore other areas such as closing the gap between design and development.
 Co-founder Jerome de Lafargue said: “zeroheight does for UX what DevOps platforms like GitHub do for building and shipping code, providing a central place to document and manage UX components, coupled with design APIs that allow teams to skip the design hand-off stage entirely and speed up the UX delivery process.”
 He said the company addresses the scaling problem for UX teams: “Problems have emerged because UX teams have grown dramatically in the past few years, because UX is now so important for most companies to just compete. And so because of this you now need centralization, you need components that are reusable so that teams can be efficient and not lose quality as it keeps shipping.”
 Zeroheight counts several Fortune 500 companies like Adobe and United Airlines as customers among its 1,300+ customer base.
title: zeroheight 筹集了由 Tribe Capital 牵头的 1000 万美元融资，用于为 UX 团队扩展 DesignOps
title_en: zeroheight raises $10M round led by Tribe Capital to scale DesignOps for UX teams

 We can’t possibly detail all of the Disrupt presentations,  speakers, events and opportunities waiting for you in one post — believe me, we’ve tried. So, let’s focus on just one aspect of Disrupt 2021 you won’t want to miss. The breakout sessions.
 You’ll find a diverse array of these sessions scattered throughout all three days. We’ll highlight a few here, and be sure to check out all of them in the  Disrupt 2021 agenda.
 Startup Pitch Feedback Sessions: You’ll find 10 of these scheduled over three days. Make time to watch and learn as all the startups exhibiting in Startup Alley pitch and hear feedback from TC staff. You’re sure to pick up tips to improve your own pitch.
 You Complete Me: In the age of the composable ecosystems, we’re all partners now – from frenemies to pure collaborations. So why is now the right time to invite friends and challengers to the table? The truth is we have to build for an unknown future, with a shared strategy and value outcome. Join us to discover five ways to encourage more symbiotic relationships in the platform economy. Presented by Elliott Limb, chief customer officer at Mambu.
 Hacking U.S. Healthcare: Few things conjure more negative emotions than navigating medical billing; Americans urgently need solutions that prioritize their needs, decrease costs and elevate the patient journey so they can focus on getting care. Digital innovation can provide exceptional patient experiences that remove friction for payers, providers and consumers. Hear how Cedar, a digital-health unicorn, engineered a consumer-first digital platform that’s revolutionizing the financial experience for the entire healthcare industry. Presented by Cedar.
  Taking Care of the Next Generation: KiwiCo empowers kids to explore, create and learn with hands-on kits. Mirvie provides a personalized window into pregnancy for early detection and intervention. Grove is committed to a plastic-free future with its line of eco-friendly beauty, home and lifestyle products. Hear from the exceptional leaders of these three companies about their mission to create a better world now and for future generations, building movements and communities, and the milestones in getting to escape velocity. Presented by Mayfield.
 TechCrunch Disrupt 2021 is where you need to be on September 21-23. Why not be there for less than $100?  Buy an early-bird pass before  the deal expires tonight  at 11:59 pm (PT).
 Is your company interested in sponsoring or exhibiting at Disrupt 2021? Contact our sponsorship sales team by   filling out this form.

根据最新公开的法庭文件，谷歌考虑收购 Epic Games，因为两家公司就 Epic 的 Fortnite Android 应用程序展开了争论。昨晚，谷歌取消了 Epic 对谷歌的反垄断投诉中的一些删节，Epic 上个月对其进行了修改和重新提交。投诉仍然省略了许多关于谷歌与特定公司交易的细节，但新的细节反映了谷歌内部关于 Android 平台竞争的沟通。 Epic 声称谷歌计划通过通过其他渠道分发 Fortnite 来回避谷歌的官方 Play 商店佣金，并且在一个未编辑的部分中，它引用了一份谷歌内部文件，称 Epic 的计划是威胁谷歌的“传染病”。以下是 Epic 对这种情况的描述：谷歌甚至与业务合作伙伴分享其垄断利润，以确保他们同意排除竞争，开发了一系列内部项目来解决它从 Epic 和其他人为消费者和开发商提供有竞争力的替代品，甚至考虑购买部分或全部 Epic 以消除这种威胁。讨论这种可能性的内部消息仍然保密，投诉并未表明谷歌曾就这些计划与 Epic 接触过。它还没有给出讨论的时间表——尽管它可能发生在 Epic 于 2018 年开始计划在 Android 上推出 Fortnite 之后。 Epic 还声称谷歌向其提供了在 Play 商店推出 Fortnite 的“特别优惠”。在另一个未密封的部分，投诉描述了一位 Google Play 经理就其侧载 Fortnite 的计划与 Epic 联系——并且显然承认侧载在这个过程中是一种“坦率地糟糕”的体验。一位经理联系了 Epic 的副总裁兼联合创始人，以评估 Epic 对一项特殊交易的兴趣，并讨论了通过直接下载“在 Android 上获得 Fortnite 的体验”。经理的通话记录指出，她认为直接下载 Fortnite 是“坦率地说是糟糕透顶的”和“糟糕的体验”，并且 Epic 应该“担心大多数人不会通过 15 个以上的步骤”。另一部分说，“工作人员在内部承认，谷歌对希望直接下载的消费者施加的困难导致了‘[p]糟糕的用户体验’，因为有 15 个以上的步骤来获得应用 [通过侧载] vs Play 或 iOS 上的 2 个步骤。'”
总体而言，“谷歌明白，它为直接下载设置的无数障碍具有保护其应用分发垄断地位和限制开发人员分发应用的能力的效果，”Epi​​c 在其投诉中称。在一份名为“对 Epic 的回应”的内部文件中，一位谷歌员工解释说，与直接下载相关的“安装摩擦”对用户来说“不仅是一种糟糕的体验”，而且谷歌“从其数据中知道它将极大地限制 [Epic 的] 抵达”。该文件继续解释说，“[f]uture [Fortnite] 更新将受到挑战：定位，通过网络更新体验”；直接下载方法“与恶意应用程序最相关”，这将“与 [Epic 的] 品牌/人口统计数据不兼容”；并且“[t] 他的方法会造成严重的用户混淆，因为 [Google Play] 仍然会吸引 [数十亿] 用户，他们将搜索 Fortnite 并遇到不清楚如何解决的死胡同”。 Epic 的诉讼最近与针对谷歌的更大的州反垄断投诉有关。与 Epic 一样，一组州检察长声称 Android 的开放程度远低于谷歌声称的那样，称其削减故意设置障碍——包括 Android 手机功能和与手机制造商的交易——以限制第三方应用商店并阻止直接下载应用。谷歌还删除了州投诉中的一些删节，主要涉及其对 Android 工作原理的内部描述。例如，在 2017 年关于亚马逊 App Store 的演讲中，谷歌显然指出，“如果我们说实话，我们会承认大多数用户和开发者并没有有意识地‘选择’他们使用默认设置。”在 2019 年，它将 Apple 和 Android 称为“新的封闭互联网生态系统”，“通过应用商店集中分发内容……[和] 通过应用商店服务进行支付”——尽管这些言论的大背景尚不清楚。谷歌强烈否认了这两起诉讼中的指控。 “开放的 Android 生态系统让开发者可以通过多个应用商店分发应用。对于选择使用 Play 商店的游戏开发商，我们有一致的政策，对开发商公平，并确保商店对用户的安全，”它在之前对 Epic 修改后的文件的评论中说。 “虽然 Fortnite 在 Android 上仍然可用，但我们不能再在 Play 上提供它，因为它违反了我们的政策。我们将继续为这些毫无根据的指控辩护。” Epic 去年对苹果和谷歌提起诉讼，因为它推出了新的 Fortnite 支付系统，回避了他们的支付系统和应用内购买佣金，导致两家公司将 Fortnite 踢出他们的移动商店。 （Fortnite 仍可通过侧载在 Android 设备上使用。）Epic 对 Apple 的诉讼于 5 月开始审理，目前正在等待法院的裁决。
note_en:
Google considered buying Epic Games as the companies sparred over Epic’s  Fortnite Android app, according to  newly unsealed court filings. Last night, Google lifted some of its redactions in Epic’s antitrust complaint against Google, which  Epic amended and refiled last month. The complaint still omits many details about Google’s dealings with specific companies, but the new details reflect internal Google communications about competition on the Android platform.
 Epic claims Google was threatened by its plans to sidestep Google’s official Play Store commission by distributing  Fortnite through other channels, and in an unredacted segment, it quotes an internal Google document calling Epic’s plans a “contagion” threatening Google. Here’s Epic’s description of the situation:
 Google has gone so far as to share its monopoly profits with business partners to secure their agreement to fence out competition, has developed a series of internal projects to address the “contagion” it perceived from efforts by Epic and others to offer consumers and developers competitive alternatives, and has even contemplated buying some or all of Epic to squelch this threat.
 The internal messages discussing that possibility remain secret, and the complaint doesn’t indicate that Google ever reached out to Epic with these plans. It also doesn’t give a timeframe for the discussion — although it presumably happened after Epic started its plans to launch  Fortnite on Android in 2018.
 Epic has also alleged that Google offered it a “special deal” to launch  Fortnite on the Play Store. In another unsealed section, the complaint describes a Google Play manager reaching out to Epic about its plans to sideload  Fortnite — and apparently admitting that sideloading is a “frankly abysmal” experience in the process.
 One manager contacted Epic’s Vice President and Co-Founder to gauge Epic’s interest in a special deal and, among other things, discussed “the experience of getting  Fortnite on Android” via direct downloading. The manager’s call notes state that she viewed direct downloading  Fortnite as “frankly abysmal” and “an awful experience”, and that Epic should “worry that most will not go through the 15+ steps.”
 Another section says that “staff members have acknowledged internally that the difficulty Google imposes on consumers who wish to direct download leads to a ‘[p]oor user experience,’ in that there are ‘15+ steps to get app [via sideloading] vs 2 steps with Play or on iOS.’”
 Overall, “Google understands that the myriad barriers it erects to direct downloading have the effect of protecting its app distribution monopoly and limiting developers’ ability to distribute their apps,” Epic alleges in its complaint.
 In an internal document titled “Response to Epic”, a Google employee explained that the “install friction” associated with direct downloading was “not only a bad experience” for users but that Google knew “from its data that it will drastically limit [Epic’s] reach”. The document goes on to explain that “[f]uture [ Fortnite] updates will be challenged re: targeting, update experience via web”; that the direct downloading approach was “most associated with malicious apps”, which would be “incompatible with [Epic’s] brand/demographics”; and that “[t]he approach will create significant user confusion, since [Google Play] will still attract [billions] of users who will search for  Fortnite and run into deadends that aren’t clear how to resolve”.
 Epic’s lawsuit was recently linked with a much bigger  state antitrust complaint against Google. Like Epic, a group of state attorneys general alleges that Android is much less open than Google claims, saying it cuts create deliberate barriers — including Android phone features and deals with phone makers — to restrict third-party app stores and discourage downloading apps directly.
  Google also removed some redactions in  the state complaints, mostly relating to its internal descriptions of how Android works. In a 2017 presentation about Amazon’s App Store, for instance, Google apparently noted that “if we were honest we would admit that most users and developers aren’t consciously ‘choosing’ they are going with the default.” And in 2019, it referred to Apple and Android as “new closed internet ecosystems” that “centralized content distribution via app stores… [and] payments via app store services” — although the larger context of those remarks isn’t clear.
 Google has strongly denied the claims in both suits. “The open Android ecosystem lets developers distribute apps through multiple app stores. For game developers who choose to use the Play Store, we have consistent policies that are fair to developers and keep the store safe for users,” it said in a previous comment on Epic’s amended filing. “While  Fortnite remains available on Android, we can no longer make it available on Play because it violates our policies. We will continue to defend ourselves against these meritless claims.”
 Epic filed lawsuits against both Apple and Google last year after it introduced a new  Fortnite payment system that sidestepped their payment systems and in-app purchase commissions, leading both companies to kick  Fortnite off their mobile stores. ( Fortnite is still available on Android devices via sideloading.) Epic’s case against Apple went to trial in May and is currently awaiting a ruling from the court.
title: 法庭文件称，谷歌考虑在 Fortnite Clash 期间购买“部分或全部”Epic
title_en: Google Considered Buying 'Some or All' of Epic During Fortnite Clash, Court Documents Say

退休精神病学家保罗·马拉里克 (Paul Malarik) 博士现在每月花大约 50 小时在他位于加利福尼亚州圣路易斯奥比斯波的家附近的临时诊所帮助注射 Covid-19 疫苗。因此，当他登录医生使用的网站 Doximity 并阅读反疫苗评论时，他尤其感到困扰。马拉里克说：“你很少能达到疫苗中微芯片的水平，但很多东西都非常接近它，”马拉里克说，他自愿花时间混合疫苗、注射疫苗和教育公众。 “他们正在积极反对我们。” Doximity 长期以来一直将自己描述为医生的 LinkedIn，它在 6 月份首次亮相股市，市值飙升至 100 亿美元。该公司在其 IPO 招股说明书中表示，它拥有 180 万会员，其中包括美国 80% 的医生。他们使用该网站相互联系、分享研究、了解行业趋势并安全地与患者沟通。在精神病学工作了二十多年的马拉里克说，仔细阅读 Doximity 的网站并发现他希望在 Facebook 和 YouTube 上看到的错误信息类型令人困惑，在那里，阴谋论猖獗。 Malarik 直接阅读了以 MD 或 DO 首字母表示的人发表的几条评论，这表示整骨医学博士，在他们的名字之后。网站上没有匿名，所以每个人都被识别出来。在帖子中，他们将疫苗称为实验性的、未经证实的或致命的，并且在谈到白宫首席医疗顾问安东尼·福奇博士时偶尔会写上“福西”。一些评论者说，感染 Covid 的抗体比信使 RNA 或 mRNA 疫苗更有效，后者指示人类细胞制造特定蛋白质，从而对疾病产生免疫反应。虽然 Covid-19 的 mRNA 疫苗目前在美国食品和药物管理局的紧急使用授权下上市，但临床试验证明它们对 Covid-19 非常有效。 FDA 和疾病控制与预防中心表示，它们是安全、有效的，推荐给 12 岁及以上的每个人，即使是那些感染过病毒的人。乔拜登总统和疾病预防控制中心主任罗谢尔瓦伦斯基博士将目前的情况描述为“未接种疫苗的大流行”。
当 Malarik 向下滚动 Doximity 新闻提要时，他停在了 6 月份的《纽约时报》故事，该故事仍然在他的页面上占据显着位置。标题写着：“一名法官驳回了休斯顿医院工作人员关于疫苗授权的诉讼。”该帖子说：“Covid-19 疫苗已经杀死了 4,000 多名接种疫苗的成年人，”这似乎是在模仿福克斯新闻主持人塔克·卡尔森（Tucker Carlson）提出的一个被揭穿的说法。 “强制使用已经杀死 4,000 多人的疫苗无异于谋杀。”这不是一个异常值。其他医生与 CNBC 分享的数十张截图和帖子描述与 Malarik 的经历一致。关于疫苗或口罩的文章有数百条评论，其中许多实际上是不准确的，而且通常基于阴谋论，而关于政治分歧较小的话题的文章只有几条评论，如果有的话。 Doximity 在首次公开募股之前基本上没有受到关注，医疗错误信息提出了一个明显的挑战，因为这家总部位于旧金山的公司寻求扩大其用户群并保持高质量可靠数据的来源，同时也在棘手的水域中航行内容审核。 Doximity 计划在下周公布上市以来的首次季度收益，此前一年的收入增长了 77%。该公司在过去三年中每年都通过降低运营成本实现盈利。 Doximity 不是一个开放的社交网络：要加入，用户必须是美国医疗保健专业人士。该公司通过医疗执照的照片识别、医院徽章、医疗机构的电子邮件以及挑战问题等方法来验证成员。与 LinkedIn 一样，该公司通过赞助内容和使用该网站寻找人才的招聘人员赚钱。由于 Doximity 完全专注于医疗专业人员，营销资金主要来自制药公司和医院，针对相关用户提供治疗和服务，包括通过新闻提要上的赞助文章和动画视频。 Doximity 上一财年超过 80% 的收入来自其营销产品。
与 LinkedIn、Facebook、Twitter 和其他流行的社交媒体平台不同，Doximity 不允许用户发布故事。该公司发布来自主流新闻媒体和医学和科学出版物的文章，每个用户的提要都是根据医疗实践领域和其他个人详细信息定制的。 “我们的平台使用算法和临床编辑器，根据会员的个人资料和阅读兴趣从各种来源中选择内容，”该公司在其招股说明书中表示。 “我们能够聚合来自各种不同来源的相关内容的连接，例如医学期刊和专业网站，否则会员可能必须单独搜索。”一个额外的吸引力是用户可以通过阅读某些符合条件的文章来获得继续医学教育学分。一些州要求医生每年获得指定数量的学分以保留他们的执照。然而，用户可以对这些故事发表评论——这就是医疗错误信息可能激增的地方。在与这些文章相同的新闻提要上，用户发现了大量的评论，但没有任何教育意义。例如，最近一篇关于为孩子们戴口罩的文章引起了一些反对疫苗的医生的愤怒。一位普通外科医生评论说：“给孩子戴面具绝对是荒谬的，是虐待儿童的一种形式。”另一位说，“CDC 和 [世界卫生组织] 积累的 50 年数据表明，这些口罩没有任何作用。没有。”科学家和公共卫生组织一再表示，口罩有助于减缓 Covid-19 的传播。 delta 变异的兴起和全国部分地区住院人数的复苏导致几个州重新制定了口罩规定。 Doximity 的规则应该限制错误信息。在其社区指南中，该公司列出了可能导致内容被删除的 11 件事，包括“传播虚假或误导性信息”。
指南页面有一个单独的部分，说明“与广泛接受的公共卫生指南相矛盾的内容”。七个要点涵盖了将被删除的帖子类型。它们包括“发布未经证实的关于使用 FDA 授权疫苗接种疫苗的有效性、副作用或影响的声明”以及“发布与传染病相关的死亡、住院、感染率的虚假数据”的内容。 Doximity 在一封电子邮件声明中表示，虽然它支持用户之间交流“关于新兴科学和最新医学新闻”的想法，但明确禁止发布医疗错误信息。该公司表示：“与大多数虚拟社区一样，我们制定了社区准则，以确保 Doximity 保持安全和相互尊重的环境。” “我们采用严格的临床审查程序，由医生负责，评估被标记为潜在错误信息的成员评论。”医生面临的风险远远超出 Doximity 采取的任何潜在行动。上周，代表全国医疗委员会的非营利组织国家医疗委员会联合会发表声明，告诉医生他们可能会失去从事此类活动的执照。 FSMB 说：“产生和传播 COVID-19 疫苗错误信息或虚假信息的医生有可能受到州医疗委员会的纪律处分，包括暂停或撤销他们的医疗执照。” “由于他们的专业知识和培训，执业医师拥有高度的公众信任，因此无论他们是否承认，在社会上都有一个强大的平台。” FSMB 表示，它正在应对医生在社交媒体和其他地方传播虚假信息的“急剧增加”。但该组织并没有积极搜索滥用者的网站。 FSMB 的发言人 Joe Knickrehm 在一封电子邮件中告诉 CNBC，州医疗委员会在“投诉驱动”系统上运作，通常会在患者、卫生系统、其他医生或公众的提示下采取行动。他说，该组织运行着一个名为 Docinfo.org 的免费工具，任何人都可以通过该工具查找有关医生的信息并提出投诉。
作为一家公司，Doximity 一直试图让用户了解 Covid-19 的发展、治疗和疫苗。在大流行初期，Doximity 启动了一个私人 Covid-19 新闻编辑室，供临床医生查找更新和建议并讨论最佳实践。它还免费提供新的视频远程医疗服务，直至 2021 年初，以帮助医生远程与患者合作。 Doximity 还拥有一个名为 Op-Med 的网站，会员可以在该网站上发布评论文章和他们的个人故事。许多医生写了一些文章，以“COVID-19 疫苗如何改变了我的生活（到目前为止）”和“接种疫苗如何重新点燃了我对行医的热爱”等标题来吹捧疫苗。但是，确定在为健康的在线辩论提供渠道和让有害的错误信息扩散之间划清界限是多年来困扰社交网络的问题。这在生死攸关的问题上尤为重要。事实上，一些反vaxxers已经认为他们被Doximity压制了。在最近对疫苗故事的评论中，一位麻醉师表示，他有机会投资 Doximity 的 IPO，其中包括向平台上的医生分配高达 15% 的资金。他写道，Doximity 审查了之前的帖子，因为它不符合公司的“疫苗接种立场”。因此，他对 IPO 股票没有兴趣。他在评论中写道：“我不会用你的思想控制牛市投资你的定向信息高速公路——”。 “祝你有美好的一天。”
title: Doximity 长期以来将自己描述为医生的 LinkedIn，其用户发现他们的新闻提要充斥着来自其他医生的反疫苗评论
title_en: Users of Doximity, which has long described itself as a LinkedIn for doctors, find their news feed inundated with anti-vaccine comments from fellow physicians
